content: Hey there! I've been thinking about getting back into speedcubing. I used to compete in local tournaments back in the Bay Area, but I've been out of practice since moving to Pittsburgh.

Welcome back to speedcubing! It's a great hobby to pick up again. How long have you been in Pittsburgh now? I'm sure there's a local community there too. Have you checked for any cubing groups or competitions nearby?

I moved here about 8 months ago for my job as a fashion buyer at a luxury department store. The work is great, but I miss the Bay Area cubing scene. I've looked around Pittsburgh, but haven't found much yet.

That's quite a change! Being a fashion buyer sounds exciting. Have you found Pittsburgh's style scene to be different from the Bay Area? And for cubing, maybe you could start a local group yourself - I bet there are others who'd be interested!

Pittsburgh has its own unique style - more classic and understated compared to the experimental fashion in SF. As for starting a cubing group, that's not a bad idea. I've also been getting into drone photography lately, which has been a fun way to explore the city.

Drone photography sounds amazing! Pittsburgh must have some great vantage points. Have you captured any particularly striking shots? And how do you balance your fashion work with these creative hobbies?

I got some incredible shots of the city skyline at sunset last week. The golden light on the rivers was breathtaking. Balancing work and hobbies is tricky though - fashion weeks are intense, and I often work late hours.

That sunset shot sounds incredible! Fashion weeks must be so demanding. Do you have any strategies for maintaining your energy and creativity during those busy periods? And have you considered combining your fashion expertise with your photography?

I try to stay energized with good nutrition and short meditation breaks during fashion weeks. As for combining fashion and photography, I've thought about doing a project on how different cities express style through street fashion. Pittsburgh would be a great start!

That street fashion project sounds fantastic! Pittsburgh's style evolution would make for a fascinating study. Have you thought about how you'd approach it - maybe starting with local fashion events or iconic neighborhoods? And do you still DJ on the side? I remember you mentioning that before.

I haven't DJed much since the move - been too focused on settling into my new role and exploring Pittsburgh. But I'm thinking of setting up my equipment again soon. Maybe I could even DJ at some local fashion events!

That would be an amazing combination - your DJ skills and fashion expertise! Have you looked into Pittsburgh's fashion event calendar? There might be some great opportunities to showcase both talents. And how's your origami coming along? I remember you were quite skilled at it.

I've actually been folding origami models to decorate my apartment - it adds a nice artistic touch. As for fashion events, I'm planning to attend the Pittsburgh Fashion Week next month. Maybe I'll bring my speedcube along and see if I can find any fellow cubers there!

That's such a creative way to use origami for home decor! And Pittsburgh Fashion Week sounds perfect for networking and maybe even starting that street fashion photography project. Will you be covering the event for your store, or is it more for personal interest?

A bit of both, actually. I'll be scouting for trends and potential designers to feature in our store, but I'm also really excited to experience it as a fashion enthusiast. Oh, and I've been playing more tennis lately too - it's a great way to meet people in a new city.

Tennis is a fantastic way to stay active and socialize in a new place! Have you joined any local clubs or found regular playing partners? And for the fashion week, are there any particular designers or trends you're most excited to see?

I joined a local tennis club and play doubles twice a week. It's been great for staying fit and making friends. As for fashion week, I'm really curious to see how Pittsburgh designers are interpreting sustainable fashion - it's becoming such an important trend in the industry.

That's wonderful that you've found a tennis community! And sustainable fashion is such a crucial movement. I'd love to hear your insights after fashion week about how Pittsburgh is approaching it. Do you think your store might start incorporating more sustainable designers based on what you see?
